pages.full_text:
tents in diferent parts of the campsite. They sing while washing up. And a play school of small children arrives with helpers to spend each day here. In one corner of the site there is a sink + French loo set apart for them.

View from our
    campsite

St. J. l. V. vinyards.
pages.notes: This page is a continuation from a previous page — text begins mid-sentence. Contains a hand-drawn sketch of what appears to be a vineyard/grapevine with a building at the top, captioned 'View from our campsite' and 'St. J. l. V. vinyards.' — 'St. J. l. V.' is an abbreviated place name, possibly St. Jean-la-Vigne or similar French village with vineyards. Some very faint/illegible text visible at bottom of page below the caption. No handwritten page number visible on left (outer top-left corner).

pages.full_text:
[illegible faint text throughout, largely obscured by drawings]

Village house R. J. l. V.
photo_captions: Village house R. J. l. V.
pages.notes: Page number 24 visible in top-right corner. The page contains multiple hand-drawn architectural sketches — five drawings of French village windows and doors with shutters, plus one sketch of a figure visible through a window, and one sketch of a doorway with decorative elements. Caption at bottom reads 'Village house R. J. l. V.' — 'R. J. l. V.' appears to be the same abbreviated location as 'St. J. l. V.' on the left page, possibly a different abbreviation form or reading error; could be same French village with vineyards. There is extensive faint handwritten text visible throughout the page behind/around the drawings, but it is almost entirely illegible due to the combination of light ink and overlapping drawings. Some purple/coloured markings visible in lower right area around the doorway sketch.
